perturbation.original_text: 1. COMMENCEMENT OF EMPLOYMENT 1.1 This Agreement shall take effect 26 September 2019 (the "Effective Date"). 1.2 Your employment shall commence on 26 September 2019 and shall continue unless and until either party gives notice to the other in accordance with paragraph 11 below. No employment with a previous employer is deemed to be continuous with your employment with the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. COMMENCEMENT OF EMPLOYMENT 1.1 This Agreement shall take effect 26 September 2019 (the "Effective Date"). 1.2 The working relationship shall commence on 26 September 2019 and shall continue unless and until either party gives notice to the other in accordance with paragraph 11 below. No employment with a previous employer is deemed to be continuous with your engagement with the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Your employment' to 'The working relationship' and 'employment' to 'engagement', the nature of the agreement becomes ambiguous. It's unclear if it's a standard employment contract or a different type of service engagement, which contradicts the implied understanding throughout the document that it is a formal employment agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. JOB TITLE 2.1 You shall serve as Chief Business Officer ("CBO") reporting to the CEO. The nature of the Company's business may result in changes occurring to the content of your role from time to time. You may also be required to carry out such additional or alternative tasks as may from time to time be reasonably required of you consistent with your executive level and job title, provided that these do not fundamentally change or undermine your position.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Your employment' to 'The working relationship' and 'employment' to 'engagement' introduces ambiguity regarding the nature of the agreement. The contradicted text specifies 'JOB TITLE' and refers to 'You shall serve as Chief Business Officer ("CBO")', implying a traditional employment relationship. However, the changed text blurs this line, potentially suggesting a contractor or consultant arrangement. This contradicts the assumption in Section 2.1 that the person is an employee with a specific job title and reporting structure, creating uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. REMUNERATION 4.1 Your salary will be USD370,000 per annum paid monthly in arrears on or about the last working day of each month (less statutory and voluntary deductions) ("Salary"). Salary will be converted to GBP and paid in GBP based on the USD/GBP Bank of England daily spot exchange rate applicable on the date of this Agreement, with the exchange rate being revised according to the prevailing Bank of England daily spot exchange rate applicable on 1 January of each year. Your Salary will be reviewed annually in accordance with the Company's practices from time to time (which is expected to be by the end of the first quarter of each year). You will be notified in writing of any changes to your Salary or benefits.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. REMUNERATION 4.1 Your compensation will be USD370,000 per annum paid monthly in arrears on or about the last working day of each month (less statutory and voluntary deductions) ("Salary"). Salary will be converted to GBP and paid in GBP based on the USD/GBP Bank of England daily spot exchange rate applicable on the date of this Agreement, with the exchange rate being revised according to the prevailing Bank of England daily spot exchange rate applicable on 1 January of each year. Your Salary will be reviewed annually in accordance with the Company's practices from time to time (which is expected to be by the end of the first quarter of each year). You will be notified in writing of any changes to your Salary or benefits.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Your salary' to 'Your compensation' introduces uncertainty regarding what constitutes 'compensation.' While 'Salary' is defined, 'compensation' is not, making it unclear whether benefits, bonuses, or equity incentives are included. This creates an in-text contradiction and impacts other parts of the contract where 'Salary' and other forms of benefits are discussed, potentially altering the overall remuneration package.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.7 If your employment is terminated on account of your death or by the Company without Cause (being for any reason not covered by paragraph 11.3), or you terminate your employment for Good Reason (as provided in paragraph 11.4), in either case outside of the Change in Control Period, then the Company shall pay you the Accrued Benefits. In addition, subject to (i) your (or your authorised representative or estate signing, if the termination is due to your death) signing a settlement agreement and a separation agreement and release (together the "Settlement Agreements") in a form and manner satisfactory to the Company, which shall include, without limitation, a general release of claims against the Company and all related persons and entities, a reaffirmation of all of your continuing obligations to the Company, including those set forth in paragraphs 13 - 15, and (in the case of the separation agreement and release) and a seven (7) business day revocation period; and (ii) the separation agreement and release becoming irrevocable, all within 60 days after the Termination Date (or such shorter period as set forth in the Settlement Agreements), the Company shall: (A) pay you (or your authorised representative or estate if the termination is due to your death) an amount equal to nine (9) months of your salary as of the Termination Date (which payment shall not be reduced by either the value of any salary paid to you during your notice period or by any payment in lieu of notice made pursuant to paragraph 11.2); and (B) pay you (or your authorised representative or estate if the termination is due to your death) an amount equal to the cost to the Company of providing you with the contractual benefits under paragraph 5 for nine (9) months or, at the Company's option, continue to provide you with such benefits for nine (9) months.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'salary' to 'compensation' introduces ambiguity. While the contract defines 'Salary', it does not define 'compensation'. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 11.7, which refers specifically to 'salary' when calculating termination payments. The contradicted text calculates termination payments based on a multiple of 'salary', and the modified text broadens 'salary' to 'compensation', raising uncertainty as to what is being multiplied (e.g., does it include benefits?). This uncertainty creates an in-text contradiction because one section expects a specific defined term (salary) and another has altered the meaning of that term by introducing a broader and undefined term (compensation).

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 In addition to the usual public holidays you will be entitled to 25 working days paid holiday in each calendar year. The holiday will accrue on a pro rata basis throughout each calendar year.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 In addition to the usual public holidays you will be entitled to 25 working days paid leave in each calendar year. The leave will accrue on a pro rata basis throughout each calendar year.
perturbation.explanation: By interchanging 'holiday' with 'leave,' an ambiguity arises, particularly considering various types of leave (sick leave, personal leave, etc.). Since 'leave' is not specifically defined in the contract, it's now open to interpretation whether the 25 days also encompass other forms of absence, creating uncertainty about the amount of time off the employee is truly entitled to for vacation versus other types of leave. This can be contradictive to clauses reffering specifically to 'sick leave' or 'Garden Leave'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.4 During any such period as described in paragraph 10.1 ("Garden Leave") the Company may appoint another person to carry out some or all of your duties. You will continue to owe all other duties and obligations (whether express or implied including fidelity and good faith) during Garden Leave and you shall continue to receive full pay and benefits (except that you will not accrue any further entitlement to any cash or equity incentive awards or bonus payments in respect of the Garden Leave period).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'holiday' to 'leave' introduces ambiguity as 'leave' is a broader term encompassing various types of leave (sick leave, personal leave, etc.) without clear definition in the contract. This directly contradicts clause 10.4 which specifies 'Garden Leave' and its associated conditions (e.g., continued duties, benefits except certain incentives). The uncertainty created by using 'leave' in 8.1 now makes it unclear whether 'Garden Leave' or other specified leaves are included within the 25 days, creating a strong inconsistency and potential for disputes regarding entitlement to different types of leave, as the term 'leave' is now not well-defined.
